description_long: This is a robust, double-sided war hammer axe with a large, rectangular metallic head featuring two broad, flat striking faces and a central, upward-pointing spike. The head is made of a marbled, gray metal with visible surface scratches and wear, suggesting heavy use or a rugged design. The handle is long, cylindrical, and appears to be made of wood, with a darker, textured grip section near the head for secure handling. At the base of the handle, there is a metal loop, possibly for hanging or attaching a strap. The overall construction is solid and utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements except for the marbled pattern on the metal. This tool is designed for heavy-duty impact, likely for demolition or combat scenarios.
description: A heavy, double-sided war hammer axe with a marbled metal head, central spike, and a long wooden handle with a metal loop at the base.
description_view_0: Front view shows the broad, rectangular metal head with two flat striking faces, central spike, and the full length of the wooden handle with a visible grip and metal loop.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the narrow profile of the axe head, the sharpness of the central spike, and the cylindrical shape of the handle.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, showing the opposite striking face, central spike, and the handle's grip and loop.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, emphasizing the thickness of the head and the alignment of the spike with the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Long, cylindrical wooden handle with a dark, textured grip section and a metal loop at the base; straight profile with visible grain.
attribute_blade_design: Rectangular, double-sided hammer head with two flat striking faces and a central, upward-pointing spike; no beard or poll, marbled metal finish.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the metal head fitted onto the wooden handle.
